brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +58.6% since Feb 27 — Brent surges to $112.57 on Houthi missile launch at Israel; WTI briefly crosses $100; strongest monthly gain since Nov 1973; +49% in March
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): Brent $112.57/bbl, +4.22% on the day. Houthi missile barrage at Israel opens third front — Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened. WTI intraday $100.04 — briefly crossed $100. Brent up ~49% in March alone — strongest monthly gain since November 1973. Goldman: $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium. Brent-WTI spread $12.93. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +49.8% since Feb 27 — WTI $99.64, intraday crossed $100; Houthi entry threatens Bab al-Mandeb; Brent-WTI spread $12.93
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): WTI $99.64, +5.46%. Intraday high $100.04 — briefly crossed $100 for first time. Houthi missile barrage at Israel opens third front. Gas at $3.98/gal national average (AAA). Brent-WTI spread $12.93. Dow enters correction territory. VIX above 30. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.00 / +33.6% since Feb 27 — $3.98 AAA avg — up $1.00 in one month — highest since Aug 2022 — EIA: $4.096/gal week ending Mar 23
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 26: $3.981/gal (+33.6% since war began). EIA weekly data: $4.096/gal for week ending Mar 23. Up $1.00 in one month — highest since August 2022. Source: AAA / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -510 pts / -7.4% — S&P 6,369; fifth straight weekly decline; Dow enters correction; VIX 31.05; CNN Fear & Greed at 'extreme fear'
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): 6,368.85, -1.67% on the day. Fifth consecutive weekly decline — longest since 2022. ~6.77% off January all-time high. Dow 45,167 entered correction territory (>10% below recent high). VIX 31.05 (+13.16%) — above 30 for first time since Mar 9. CNN Fear & Greed Index at 'extreme fear.' Energy only positive S&P sector since war. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNN.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 10,000+ targets — 850+ Tomahawks expended — 82nd Airborne deploying — Houthi third front opens — $50-200B supplemental
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 29: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets, 8,000+ combat flights, 850+ Tomahawks expended in first month. 82nd Airborne deploying. Houthis open third front with missile barrage at Israel. Trump extended Hormuz deadline to Apr 6. Pentagon preparing $50-200B supplemental funding request. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (29 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$34.0-34.6B — $50-200B supplemental proposed
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 29: ~$34.0-34.6B. Trump preparing $50-200B supplemental funding request. Rubio told G7 war could last 'another 2 to 4 weeks.' Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Replenishment: $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 10,000+ US targets — 8,000+ combat flights — 850+ Tomahawks — 92% of large naval vessels (150+) destroyed — 330/470 ballistic launchers destroyed/disabled
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 28): 10,000+ US targets, 8,000+ combat flights, 850+ Tomahawk missiles expended. 92% of Iran's large naval vessels destroyed — over 150 sunk — 'largest elimination of a navy since WWII.' Israeli assessment: 330 of ~470 ballistic missile launchers destroyed or disabled. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~29,500+ through Day 29. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Israeli assessments.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 US KIA total — 6 Kuwait (Mar 1), 1 Saudi (Sgt. Pennington), 6 KC-135 crash (Mar 12) — 300+ WIA per CENTCOM
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 US KIA total: 6 in Kuwait drone attack (Mar 1), 1 from injuries in Saudi strike (Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26), 6 in KC-135 crash in western Iraq (Mar 12). 300+ wounded per CENTCOM (Mar 27-28). 10 US troops injured in Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia (Mar 27-28). 82nd Airborne deploying. Source: CENTCOM / Department of War / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 300+ total per CENTCOM — 10 injured at Prince Sultan AB (Mar 27-28) — 50,000+ deployed + 2,000 82nd ABN deploying
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 300+ WIA per CENTCOM (Mar 27-28). At least 10 US troops injured in Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, which also damaged refueling aircraft. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR plus ~2,000 82nd Airborne deploying. Trump considering 10,000 additional troops. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: HRANA: 3,114 (1,354 civ, 217 children) — WaPo: ~1,500 civilians — Hengaw: 5,300+ military — Iran deputy UN rep: 1,750 — wide discrepancy
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. HRANA (US-based, Mar 17): 3,114 documented (1,354 civilian, 1,138 military, 622 unclassified). Washington Post: ~1,500 Iranian civilians killed. Hengaw: 5,300+ military killed by Mar 18. Iran deputy UN rep: 1,750 total. Independent verification severely hampered by internet shutdowns. 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones fired by Iran. Source: HRANA / WaPo / Hengaw / Iran UN Mission.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,116+ killed — 3,229 injured — 1.2M displaced (1 in 5 residents) — Division 162 expanding buffer zone — 750+ Hezbollah fighters killed per IDF
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,116+ killed, 3,229 injured per Lebanese MoH (Mar 26). 1.2 million displaced — one in five residents. IDF claims 750+ Hezbollah fighters killed. Division 162 deployed to expand buffer zone in western sector. IDF ordered destruction of all Litani River crossings. PM Salam banned Hezbollah military activities, expelled Iran ambassador. Hezbollah escalated to 600 rockets/missiles/drones in single 24-hour period. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / Al Jazeera.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1.2M — 2,000 vessels + 20,000 seafarers stranded — Houthi entry threatens Red Sea route
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2M (UNHCR). Lebanon: 1.2M (one in five residents). Nearly 2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers stranded near Strait of Hormuz. Houthi entry into war threatens Bab al-Mandeb — could compound Hormuz closure. 30 tankers staged near Yanbu within Houthi strike range. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 2,000 vessels stranded — 20,000 seafarers — Houthi third front opens — Bab al-Mandeb threatened
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Nearly 2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers stranded near Hormuz. Houthis launched first missile barrage at Israel (Mar 28) — threatened to close Bab al-Mandeb strait. ~30 tankers near Yanbu within Houthi range. Red Sea traffic already 'sharply reduced' (Windward). Maersk paused trans-Suez sailings. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 217+ (HRANA) + Lebanon 118+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 150-170+ killed (mostly schoolchildren) — UNHRC investigating
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 217 children confirmed in Iran. At least 217 children killed in Iran per HRANA. Amnesty verified Minab school: 150-170+ killed (mostly schoolchildren aged 7-12, girls' elementary school) — US Tomahawk fragments confirmed — outdated DIA intel. UN Human Rights Council investigating as potential war crime. Lebanon: 118+ children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Source: UNICEF / Amnesty / HRANA / UNHRC.
